title: pinus edulis var. monophylla
intro: Pinus edulis var. monophylla, also known as the Mexican nut pine or piñon pine, is a type of small evergreen tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its edible seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that may aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The seeds of Pinus edulis var. monophylla have been traditionally used to support respiratory health, lower cholesterol levels, and provide antioxidant benefits. The seeds are often harvested, dried, and used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, offering a natural remedy for various health concerns.
